refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure operational availability meets or exceeds the customer-defined target by minimizing downtime and enabling rapid recovery from failures.
refined_fr.rationale: Availability is a high-level KPI that depends on minimizing system downtime and ensuring quick recovery. Refining the FR into sub-FRs targeting these aspects allows focused design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure system uptime and downtime over the evaluation period; verify recovery time from simulated failures meets target thresholds.
sub_frs: Minimize unplanned downtime of the NGHE solution
Enable rapid recovery and fault tolerance in the NGHE solution

candidate_dps: Redundant system components and failover mechanisms
Robust fault detection and recovery algorithms
selected_dp.selected_dp: Redundant system components and failover mechanisms

potential_sources: Dependency between fault detection and failover logic
Interaction between redundancy management and teleoperation control
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates availability concerns via redundancy, minimizing coupling with other functional domains.

assumptions_risks: Assumes redundancy can be implemented within existing hardware and software constraints
Risk that failover mechanisms may introduce latency impacting operator control
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating predictive maintenance DP in next refinement level to further improve availability.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and embedded systems responsible for operational continuity and failover

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Reports system health and status to NGHE Solution and Safety Services

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication for failover coordination and remote monitoring

module_containment_scores.notes: High diffusion due to system-wide coordination, but containment is moderate as redundancy is mostly local to Vehicle.
Primary containment of redundancy and failover logic; low diffusion outside Vehicle.
Limited containment; mainly receives status updates.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Redundancy and failover mechanisms are best contained within the Vehicle module to minimize diffusion and enable rapid local recovery.
containment_risks: Potential cross-module coordination delays if failover requires external module interaction
Risk of incomplete fault coverage if some redundancy aspects are outside Vehicle
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate if some failover coordination should be delegated to NGHE Solution module to improve system-wide availability.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR by addressing availability through redundancy and failover.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion into NGHE Solution module due to system-wide coordination
Potential interface complexity on Network Link
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment within Vehicle is strong, minimizing diffusion and supporting ownership clarity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and fault injection tests localized to Vehicle module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of availability responsibility to Vehicle module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Consider next-level refinement to incorporate predictive maintenance DP and its IM assignment.
Review interface definitions for failover coordination to reduce diffusion.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition is sound, incorporating predictive maintenance and fault detection in this level would strengthen the availability solution and reduce risks.
next_level_sub_frs: Minimize unplanned downtime of the NGHE solution
Enable rapid recovery and fault tolerance in the NGHE solution

revise.why: To explicitly include predictive maintenance and fault detection as sub-FRs for a more comprehensive availability strategy.

assumptions: Redundancy can be implemented within Vehicle module constraints
Failover mechanisms will not adversely impact operator control latency
open_risks: Incomplete fault coverage if redundancy is fragmented
evidence_needed: Test results from fault injection and recovery scenarios
